db.db_description: This is a healthcare research database implemented in the OMOP Common Data Model (CDM) version 5.3.1, populated from a Synthea synthetic patient population. It standardizes longitudinal clinical data for 2,694 patients (born 1909–1985) across the full set of OMOP clinical event tables — person demographics, observation periods, visit occurrences (all coded as inpatient hospitalizations), condition occurrences and eras, drug exposures and eras, procedures, measurements, observations, and deaths — spanning records from roughly 1910 to 2019. It includes the standardized OMOP vocabulary tables (concept, concept_relationship, concept_ancestor, concept_synonym, vocabulary, domain, relationship, drug_strength) that map and hierarchically organize standardized clinical concepts drawn from terminologies such as SNOMED, RxNorm, LOINC, ICD9CM, and UCUM, plus health-system reference tables (care_site, provider, location). Several optional/derived tables (cost, payer_plan_period, attribute_definition, concept_class, metadata, fact_relationship) are present but empty. The database's purpose is to support standardized analytics and observational research on synthetic patient health records.

attribute_definition.table_description: A metadata/lookup table from the OMOP CDM defining named attributes (e.g., cohort or measurement attributes), with each row describing one attribute, its meaning, type, and the logic/syntax used to derive it. The table is currently empty (0 rows).

columns.name: attribute_definition_id
columns.description: Unique integer identifier (primary key) for each attribute definition.

columns.name: attribute_name
columns.description: Human-readable short name/label of the attribute.

columns.name: attribute_description
columns.description: Free-text description explaining the meaning or purpose of the attribute.

columns.name: attribute_type_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to a vocabulary/concept table; concept id encoding the type or source of the attribute definition (e.g., how it was constructed).

columns.name: attribute_syntax
columns.description: Free-text logic or syntax (e.g., SQL or rule expression) used to compute/derive the attribute value.

care_site.table_description: OMOP CDM CARE_SITE table. Each row represents a care site — a uniquely identified institutional (physical or organizational) unit where healthcare is delivered, such as a hospital, clinic, ward, or physician office.

columns.name: care_site_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each care site.

columns.name: care_site_name
columns.description: Free-text name of the care site (e.g., hospital or clinic name).

columns.name: place_of_service_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the OMOP CONCEPT table; standardized concept identifying the place of service / type of care site (e.g., inpatient, outpatient, emergency).

columns.name: location_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the OMOP LOCATION table giving the physical/geographic address of the care site.

columns.name: care_site_source_value
columns.description: The care site identifier or name as it appears in the source data, prior to mapping.

columns.name: place_of_service_source_value
columns.description: The place-of-service value as represented in the source data, prior to mapping to place_of_service_concept_id.

cdm_source.table_description: OMOP Common Data Model (CDM) metadata table holding a single row that describes the source dataset and ETL provenance for this CDM instance — here a Synthea synthetic patient population converted to OMOP CDM v5.3.1.

columns.name: cdm_source_name
columns.description: Full human-readable name of the source dataset/database (e.g., 'Synthea synthetic health database').

columns.name: cdm_source_abbreviation
columns.description: Short name/abbreviation for the source (e.g., 'Synthea').

columns.name: cdm_holder
columns.description: Organization or entity that holds/maintains this CDM instance (e.g., 'OHDSI Community').

columns.name: source_description
columns.description: Free-text narrative describing the source data and its purpose.

columns.name: source_documentation_reference
columns.description: URL or citation pointing to documentation of the original source data.

columns.name: cdm_etl_reference
columns.description: URL or citation referencing the ETL process/code used to transform the source into the OMOP CDM (e.g., GitHub repo).

columns.name: source_release_date
columns.description: Date the source/raw data was released.

columns.name: cdm_release_date
columns.description: Date this OMOP CDM instance was generated/released.

columns.name: cdm_version
columns.description: Version of the OMOP Common Data Model used (e.g., 'v5.3.1').

columns.name: vocabulary_version
columns.description: Version/release identifier of the OMOP standardized vocabularies used (e.g., 'v5.0 18-JAN-19').

cohort_definition.table_description: An OMOP CDM metadata table that defines named cohorts: each row records the definition of a cohort (a set of subjects meeting specified criteria), including its name, description, the syntax/logic used to construct it, and what kind of entity the subjects are.

columns.name: cohort_definition_id
columns.description: Unique integer identifier for the cohort definition; serves as the primary key and is referenced by cohort membership tables.

columns.name: cohort_definition_name
columns.description: Short human-readable name/title of the cohort definition.

columns.name: cohort_definition_description
columns.description: Optional free-text description explaining the purpose or criteria of the cohort.

columns.name: definition_type_concept_id
columns.description: Concept ID (references the OMOP standardized vocabulary/CONCEPT table) indicating the type or method of the cohort definition, e.g. whether defined by SQL, ATLAS, or external tool.

columns.name: cohort_definition_syntax
columns.description: Free text holding the actual syntax/logic (e.g. SQL query or JSON definition) used to construct the cohort.

columns.name: subject_concept_id
columns.description: Concept ID (references CONCEPT) indicating the domain/type of the cohort subjects, e.g. whether subjects are persons, providers, or visits.

columns.name: cohort_initiation_date
columns.description: Optional date on which the cohort definition was created or first instantiated.

concept.table_description: OMOP Common Data Model CONCEPT table: a standardized vocabulary dictionary where each row is a distinct clinical concept (drug, condition, measurement, procedure, etc.) drawn from source vocabularies like RxNorm, SNOMED, and LOINC.

columns.name: concept_id
columns.description: Unique integer surrogate key identifying each standardized concept (OMOP concept_id); primary key, 100% distinct.

columns.name: concept_name
columns.description: Human-readable name/label of the concept, e.g. 'celecoxib 200 MG Oral Capsule [Celebrex]' or 'Albumin serum/plasma'.

columns.name: domain_id
columns.description: Code for the OMOP domain the concept belongs to, e.g. Drug, Condition, Measurement, Procedure, Observation, Visit.

columns.name: vocabulary_id
columns.description: Identifier of the source vocabulary the concept originates from, e.g. RxNorm, SNOMED, LOINC, CVX, Visit.

columns.name: concept_class_id
columns.description: Code classifying the concept within its vocabulary, e.g. Clinical Finding, Ingredient, Clinical Drug, Procedure, Lab Test, 11-digit NDC.

columns.name: standard_concept
columns.description: Flag indicating whether the concept is a standard concept; 'S' for standard, null otherwise (no 'C'/classification values present in this sample).

columns.name: concept_code
columns.description: The original code for the concept in its source vocabulary (e.g. RxNorm code '310965', LOINC '1751-7', NDC '00025152531'); unique per row here.

columns.name: valid_start_date
columns.description: Date on which the concept became valid in the vocabulary; defaults to 1970-01-01 for long-standing concepts.

columns.name: valid_end_date
columns.description: Date on which the concept stops being valid; all rows set to the sentinel 2099-12-31 indicating still currently valid.

columns.name: invalid_reason
columns.description: Reason a concept was deprecated (e.g. 'D' deleted, 'U' updated); entirely null here since all concepts are valid.

concept_ancestor.table_description: OMOP Common Data Model CONCEPT_ANCESTOR table: a precomputed hierarchical lookup that records every ancestor/descendant relationship between standardized concepts, capturing the transitive closure of the vocabulary hierarchy along with the distance between each pair.

columns.name: ancestor_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to CONCEPT.concept_id identifying the ancestor (more general/higher-level) standardized concept in the hierarchy.

columns.name: descendant_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to CONCEPT.concept_id identifying the descendant (more specific/lower-level) standardized concept subsumed under the ancestor.

columns.name: min_levels_of_separation
columns.description: Minimum number of hierarchy levels (shortest path of subsumption hops) between the ancestor and descendant concepts; 0 means the concept is its own ancestor.

columns.name: max_levels_of_separation
columns.description: Maximum number of hierarchy levels (longest path of subsumption hops) between the ancestor and descendant concepts.

concept_class.table_description: OMOP CDM vocabulary reference table that defines concept classes — categories used to group standardized concepts within a vocabulary (e.g., 'Clinical Finding', 'Ingredient'). Currently empty (0 rows).

columns.name: concept_class_id
columns.description: Short string identifier/code for the concept class (primary key); a unique key referenced by CONCEPT.concept_class_id.

columns.name: concept_class_name
columns.description: Human-readable name/label describing the concept class.

columns.name: concept_class_concept_id
columns.description: Integer foreign key to the CONCEPT table identifying the concept that represents this concept class itself.

concept_relationship.table_description: An OMOP CDM vocabulary table recording directed relationships between pairs of standardized concepts (e.g., "Maps to", "Is a", "Subsumes"), with validity dates for each relationship.

columns.name: concept_id_1
columns.description: Source concept identifier in the relationship; references a concept_id in the CONCEPT table.

columns.name: concept_id_2
columns.description: Target/destination concept identifier in the relationship; references a concept_id in the CONCEPT table.

columns.name: relationship_id
columns.description: Code/name of the relationship type linking the two concepts (e.g., 'Maps to', 'Mapped from', 'Is a', 'Subsumes'); references the RELATIONSHIP vocabulary.

columns.name: valid_start_date
columns.description: Date on which the relationship became valid.

columns.name: valid_end_date
columns.description: Date on which the relationship ceases to be valid; default 2099-12-31 indicates currently active.

columns.name: invalid_reason
columns.description: Reason the relationship is no longer valid (e.g., 'D' deleted, 'U' updated); NULL when the relationship is currently valid.

concept_synonym.table_description: OMOP Common Data Model CONCEPT_SYNONYM table: each row records an alternative name (synonym) for a standardized clinical concept, allowing multiple textual representations per concept.

columns.name: concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to concept.concept_id; identifies the standardized vocabulary concept that this synonym describes. Repeated values (distinct ratio 0.43) indicate a concept can have multiple synonyms.

columns.name: concept_synonym_name
columns.description: The alternative/synonym text for the concept (e.g. 'Sprain of ankle', 'Acute cholecystitis (disorder)'). Free-text string, unique per row.

columns.name: language_concept_id
columns.description: Concept ID (FK to concept) indicating the language of the synonym text. All rows use the single value 4180186, the OMOP concept for English.

person.table_description: OMOP Common Data Model PERSON table: each row is a distinct individual patient, recording demographic attributes (gender, birth date, race, ethnicity) as both source values and standardized concept IDs. Here populated with 2,694 patients born 1909–1985.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Unique integer primary key identifying each person in the database.

columns.name: gender_concept_id
columns.description: Standardized OMOP concept ID for the person's gender (FK to concept.concept_id); observed values 8507=MALE, 8532=FEMALE.

columns.name: year_of_birth
columns.description: Year of birth as a 4-digit integer (range 1909–1985).

columns.name: month_of_birth
columns.description: Month of birth as an integer 1–12; nullable.

columns.name: day_of_birth
columns.description: Day of the month of birth as an integer 1–31; nullable.

columns.name: birth_datetime
columns.description: Full date/time of birth as a timestamp (time component always midnight); nullable.

columns.name: race_concept_id
columns.description: Standardized OMOP concept ID for race (FK to concept.concept_id); 0 indicates unmapped/no matching standard concept (e.g. 8527=White, 8516=Black/African American, 8515=Asian).

columns.name: ethnicity_concept_id
columns.description: Standardized OMOP concept ID for ethnicity (FK to concept.concept_id); 38003563=Hispanic or Latino, 0=Not Hispanic/unmapped.

columns.name: location_id
columns.description: FK to a location/address record for the person; entirely NULL in this dataset.

columns.name: provider_id
columns.description: FK to the person's primary care provider; entirely NULL in this dataset.

columns.name: care_site_id
columns.description: FK to the person's primary care site/facility; entirely NULL in this dataset.

columns.name: person_source_value
columns.description: Original source identifier for the person, stored as a UUID string; unique per person.

columns.name: gender_source_value
columns.description: Gender as recorded in the source data, single-character code 'M' or 'F'.

columns.name: gender_source_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept ID for the source gender code (FK to concept.concept_id); always 0 (unmapped) here.

columns.name: race_source_value
columns.description: Race as recorded in the source data, free-text lowercase labels (white, hispanic, black, asian, native).

columns.name: race_source_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept ID for the source race code (FK to concept.concept_id); always 0 (unmapped) here.

columns.name: ethnicity_source_value
columns.description: Ethnicity/nationality as recorded in source data, free-text lowercase labels (e.g. irish, italian, german, puerto_rican, west_indian).

columns.name: ethnicity_source_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept ID for the source ethnicity code (FK to concept.concept_id); always 0 (unmapped) here.

condition_era.table_description: OMOP Common Data Model CONDITION_ERA table: each row is a continuous span (era) during which a patient is inferred to have a given clinical condition, aggregated from underlying condition occurrences. Contains 65,719 eras across 2,694 patients.

columns.name: condition_era_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each condition era; integer, unique per row (distinct_ratio 1.0).

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Foreign key to PERSON.person_id identifying the patient the condition era belongs to.

columns.name: condition_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to CONCEPT.concept_id holding a standardized condition concept (typically SNOMED-based) identifying the diagnosed condition; 0 denotes an unmapped/unknown concept.

columns.name: condition_era_start_date
columns.description: Start date of the condition era (earliest date the condition was observed in this span); ranges 1912–2019.

columns.name: condition_era_end_date
columns.description: End date of the condition era (latest date the condition persisted in this span); ranges 1912–2019.

columns.name: condition_occurrence_count
columns.description: Number of individual condition occurrences rolled up into this era; all sampled values are 1, indicating eras built from single occurrences here.

condition_occurrence.table_description: OMOP Common Data Model CONDITION_OCCURRENCE table: each row records a single diagnosed condition/disease for a patient, with start/end dates, standardized condition concepts, and source codes. Here populated with 65,332 condition records linked to ~616 patients.

columns.name: condition_occurrence_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each condition occurrence record.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Foreign key to person.person_id identifying the patient who has the condition.

columns.name: condition_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to concept.concept_id; standardized condition concept (typically a SNOMED-based OMOP diagnosis concept).

columns.name: condition_start_date
columns.description: Date the condition was first recorded or began (range 1917–2019).

columns.name: condition_start_datetime
columns.description: Timestamp of the condition start; mirrors condition_start_date with 00:00:00 time component.

columns.name: condition_end_date
columns.description: Date the condition resolved or ended; null (~14%) when ongoing or unknown.

columns.name: condition_end_datetime
columns.description: Timestamp of the condition end; mirrors condition_end_date, null when not applicable.

columns.name: condition_type_concept_id
columns.description: Concept ID indicating the provenance/type of the condition record; constant 32020 here (a single source type).

columns.name: condition_status_concept_id
columns.description: Concept ID for diagnosis status; all values 0 (unmapped/not specified) in this data.

columns.name: stop_reason
columns.description: Free-text reason the condition ended; entirely null in this dataset.

columns.name: provider_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the provider associated with the diagnosis; entirely null here.

columns.name: visit_occurrence_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the visit (visit_occurrence) during which the condition was recorded.

columns.name: visit_detail_id
columns.description: Foreign key to a finer-grained visit detail; all values 0 (not populated).

columns.name: condition_source_value
columns.description: Original source diagnosis code as recorded, e.g. SNOMED numeric codes or ICD-10 codes like 'K92.2'.

columns.name: condition_source_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to concept.concept_id representing the source code's concept; mirrors condition_concept_id values here.

columns.name: condition_status_source_value
columns.description: Original source value describing diagnosis status; entirely null in this dataset.

cost.table_description: OMOP CDM COST table: records the financial cost, charges, and payment breakdowns associated with clinical events (e.g., drug exposures, procedures, visits). Currently empty (0 rows).

columns.name: cost_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each cost record.

columns.name: cost_event_id
columns.description: Identifier of the clinical event the cost is attached to; references the record (e.g., drug_exposure_id, procedure_occurrence_id, visit_occurrence_id) in the domain given by cost_domain_id.

columns.name: cost_domain_id
columns.description: Domain string indicating which event table cost_event_id refers to (e.g., 'Drug', 'Procedure', 'Visit', 'Device').

columns.name: cost_type_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept ID for the type/provenance of the cost record (e.g., source of cost data).

columns.name: currency_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept ID identifying the currency in which the monetary amounts are expressed (e.g., USD, EUR).

columns.name: total_charge
columns.description: Total amount charged/billed by the provider for the event, in the given currency.

columns.name: total_cost
columns.description: Total cost incurred to produce the service for the event, in the given currency.

columns.name: total_paid
columns.description: Total amount actually paid for the event (payer plus patient), in the given currency.

columns.name: paid_by_payer
columns.description: Portion of the total paid amount covered by the payer/insurer.

columns.name: paid_by_patient
columns.description: Portion of the total paid amount paid out-of-pocket by the patient.

columns.name: paid_patient_copay
columns.description: Patient out-of-pocket amount attributed to copayment.

columns.name: paid_patient_coinsurance
columns.description: Patient out-of-pocket amount attributed to coinsurance.

columns.name: paid_patient_deductible
columns.description: Patient out-of-pocket amount applied to the deductible.

columns.name: paid_by_primary
columns.description: Amount paid by the primary payer when multiple payers are involved (coordination of benefits).

columns.name: paid_ingredient_cost
columns.description: For drug costs, the amount paid for the drug ingredient itself.

columns.name: paid_dispensing_fee
columns.description: For drug costs, the amount paid as the pharmacy dispensing fee.

columns.name: payer_plan_period_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the payer_plan_period table identifying the patient's insurance plan coverage period for this cost.

columns.name: amount_allowed
columns.description: Contractually allowed amount the payer agrees to reimburse for the event.

columns.name: revenue_code_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept ID for the revenue code categorizing the service line (institutional billing).

columns.name: revenue_code_source_value
columns.description: Source/original revenue code value as it appears in the raw billing data.

columns.name: drg_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept ID for the Diagnosis Related Group (DRG) associated with the cost/admission.

columns.name: drg_source_value
columns.description: Source/original DRG code value as it appears in the raw billing data.

death.table_description: OMOP CDM DEATH table: records the date and cause of death for a person. One row per death event, linked to a person.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the PERSON table identifying the deceased individual.

columns.name: death_date
columns.description: Calendar date on which the person died.

columns.name: death_datetime
columns.description: Date and time of death (no timezone); optional, more precise than death_date.

columns.name: death_type_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP standard concept id describing the provenance/type of the death record (e.g., EHR, death registry, payer claim).

columns.name: cause_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP standard concept id for the cause of death (mapped SNOMED/condition concept).

columns.name: cause_source_value
columns.description: Source code string for the cause of death as recorded in the source system (e.g., ICD code).

columns.name: cause_source_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept id corresponding to the source-system cause-of-death code prior to standard mapping.

device_exposure.table_description: OMOP CDM DEVICE_EXPOSURE table: records each instance of a patient's exposure to or use of a foreign physical object or instrument (e.g., implants, devices, supplies) during healthcare, including timing, source coding, and the associated visit/provider.

columns.name: device_exposure_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each device exposure record.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the PERSON table identifying the patient exposed to the device.

columns.name: device_concept_id
columns.description: Standard OMOP concept ID identifying the type of device used/exposed.

columns.name: device_exposure_start_date
columns.description: Date the device exposure began.

columns.name: device_exposure_start_datetime
columns.description: Date and time the device exposure began.

columns.name: device_exposure_end_date
columns.description: Date the device exposure ended.

columns.name: device_exposure_end_datetime
columns.description: Date and time the device exposure ended.

columns.name: device_type_concept_id
columns.description: Standard OMOP concept ID indicating the provenance/type of the source record (e.g., EHR, claim) for the device exposure.

columns.name: unique_device_id
columns.description: Unique Device Identifier (UDI) string assigned to the specific physical device, when available.

columns.name: quantity
columns.description: Number of devices used/dispensed in this exposure.

columns.name: provider_id
columns.description: Foreign key to PROVIDER table for the clinician associated with the device exposure.

columns.name: visit_occurrence_id
columns.description: Foreign key to VISIT_OCCURRENCE table for the visit during which the device was used.

columns.name: visit_detail_id
columns.description: Foreign key to VISIT_DETAIL table for the visit detail during which the device was used.

columns.name: device_source_value
columns.description: Original source code/text for the device as represented in the source data, prior to mapping.

columns.name: device_source_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept ID representing the device code in the source vocabulary before mapping to a standard concept.

domain.table_description: OMOP CDM vocabulary reference table enumerating the set of clinical data domains (e.g., Condition, Drug, Procedure, Measurement, Visit). Each row defines one domain used to classify standard concepts.

columns.name: domain_id
columns.description: Unique short textual key identifying the domain (e.g., 'Condition', 'Procedure', 'Drug/Obs', 'Modifier', 'Visit'). Primary key.

columns.name: domain_name
columns.description: Human-readable name/label of the domain, sometimes with a descriptive phrase (e.g., 'Measurement Value', 'Payer - institution administering healthcare transactions').

columns.name: domain_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign-key concept_id (into the CONCEPT table) that represents this domain as a standardized concept.

dose_era.table_description: OMOP CDM DOSE_ERA table: each row represents a continuous span of time during which a person was exposed to a constant dose of a specific active ingredient. Derived (era) table aggregating drug exposures by ingredient and dose level.

columns.name: dose_era_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key; unique identifier for each dose era record.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Identifier of the person this dose era belongs to; foreign key to the PERSON table.

columns.name: drug_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP standard concept id for the drug ingredient (RxNorm ingredient-level concept) the dose era applies to.

columns.name: unit_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP standard concept id for the unit of measure of the dose value (e.g., mg, mL).

columns.name: dose_value
columns.description: Numeric dose amount of the ingredient that was constant throughout the era, expressed in the unit given by unit_concept_id.

columns.name: dose_era_start_date
columns.description: Start date of the period of continuous exposure at this constant dose.

columns.name: dose_era_end_date
columns.description: End date of the period of continuous exposure at this constant dose.

drug_era.table_description: OMOP Common Data Model DRUG_ERA table: each row is a continuous span (era) during which a patient is inferred to be exposed to a single standardized (RxNorm ingredient) drug, derived by aggregating individual drug exposures. Contains 52,508 eras for 2,694 patients.

columns.name: drug_era_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each drug era record.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Foreign key to person.person_id; the patient this drug era belongs to.

columns.name: drug_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to concept.concept_id; the standardized drug concept (typically an RxNorm ingredient) the patient was exposed to during this era.

columns.name: drug_era_start_date
columns.description: Date the inferred continuous drug exposure era began (earliest exposure). Ranges 1921–2019.

columns.name: drug_era_end_date
columns.description: Date the inferred continuous drug exposure era ended (latest exposure plus persistence window).

columns.name: drug_exposure_count
columns.description: Count of individual drug exposure records aggregated into this era (observed values 1–4).

columns.name: gap_days
columns.description: Number of days within the era not covered by drug exposure (persistence gap). Values here span large positive and negative ranges, suggesting non-standard or miscomputed population of this OMOP field.

drug_exposure.table_description: OMOP Common Data Model DRUG_EXPOSURE table: each row records a drug/medication administered, prescribed, or dispensed to a patient over a time interval, linking the patient, the standardized drug concept, and source codes. Here 67,707 exposures across 2,694 patients from 1913 to 2019.

columns.name: drug_exposure_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each drug exposure record.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Foreign key to person.person_id; the patient who received the drug.

columns.name: drug_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to concept.concept_id; standardized drug concept (RxNorm) for the administered/dispensed drug.

columns.name: drug_exposure_start_date
columns.description: Date the drug exposure began.

columns.name: drug_exposure_start_datetime
columns.description: Timestamp the drug exposure began; times are all 00:00:00, mirroring start_date.

columns.name: drug_exposure_end_date
columns.description: Date the drug exposure ended.

columns.name: drug_exposure_end_datetime
columns.description: Timestamp the drug exposure ended; times all 00:00:00, mirroring end_date.

columns.name: verbatim_end_date
columns.description: End date as originally recorded in the source data, before any ETL derivation; null in ~10% of rows.

columns.name: drug_type_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to concept.concept_id; standardized type/provenance of the drug record (e.g., prescription written vs. dispensed). Only two values present: 38000177 and 581452.

columns.name: stop_reason
columns.description: Free-text reason the drug was stopped; entirely null in this dataset.

columns.name: refills
columns.description: Number of refills authorized/recorded; constant 0 here.

columns.name: quantity
columns.description: Quantity of drug dispensed/administered; constant 0 here.

columns.name: days_supply
columns.description: Number of days the dispensed supply is expected to last; ranges 0 to 6794.

columns.name: sig
columns.description: Free-text dosing instructions (signa); entirely null in this dataset.

columns.name: route_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to concept.concept_id; standardized route of administration. Constant 0 (unmapped/no value) here.

columns.name: lot_number
columns.description: Manufacturer lot number of the drug product; constant '0' (unpopulated) here.

columns.name: provider_id
columns.description: Foreign key to a provider; the prescriber/administering clinician. Constant 0 (unpopulated) here.

columns.name: visit_occurrence_id
columns.description: Foreign key to a visit_occurrence; the encounter during which the drug was given. Nearly all populated.

columns.name: visit_detail_id
columns.description: Foreign key to a visit_detail (sub-visit); constant 0 (unpopulated) here.

columns.name: drug_source_value
columns.description: Original drug code as recorded in the source system (e.g., NDC or RxNorm code strings).

columns.name: drug_source_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to concept.concept_id; the source-vocabulary drug concept before standardization. Values match drug_concept_id here.

columns.name: route_source_value
columns.description: Original route-of-administration text from the source; entirely null here.

columns.name: dose_unit_source_value
columns.description: Original dose unit text from the source; entirely null here.

drug_strength.table_description: OMOP CDM vocabulary table that records the strength of each drug product in terms of its active ingredients, expressed either as an absolute amount or as a concentration (numerator/denominator). Each row links a drug concept to one of its ingredient concepts with the associated quantity and units.

columns.name: drug_concept_id
columns.description: Reference to the CONCEPT id of the drug product (e.g. branded/clinical drug) whose strength is described. FK to CONCEPT.concept_id.

columns.name: ingredient_concept_id
columns.description: Reference to the CONCEPT id of the active ingredient contained in the drug. FK to CONCEPT.concept_id.

columns.name: amount_value
columns.description: Numeric absolute quantity of the ingredient in the drug product (used for solid/discrete forms such as tablets), measured in amount_unit_concept_id units.

columns.name: amount_unit_concept_id
columns.description: Reference to the CONCEPT id giving the unit of measure for amount_value (e.g. mg). FK to CONCEPT.concept_id.

columns.name: numerator_value
columns.description: Numeric numerator quantity of the ingredient when strength is expressed as a concentration/ratio (e.g. amount of active ingredient per volume).

columns.name: numerator_unit_concept_id
columns.description: Reference to the CONCEPT id giving the unit of measure for numerator_value (e.g. mg). FK to CONCEPT.concept_id.

columns.name: denominator_value
columns.description: Numeric denominator quantity defining the concentration's reference quantity (e.g. per mL of solution).

columns.name: denominator_unit_concept_id
columns.description: Reference to the CONCEPT id giving the unit of measure for denominator_value (e.g. mL). FK to CONCEPT.concept_id.

columns.name: box_size
columns.description: Number of units (e.g. tablets, vials) contained in the marketed box/package of the drug product.

columns.name: valid_start_date
columns.description: Date on which this drug-strength record became valid in the vocabulary.

columns.name: valid_end_date
columns.description: Date on which this drug-strength record ceased to be valid (often a far-future sentinel for active records).

columns.name: invalid_reason
columns.description: Code indicating why the record is no longer valid (e.g. 'D' deprecated, 'U' updated); NULL when currently valid.

fact_relationship.table_description: OMOP CDM FACT_RELATIONSHIP table: records directional relationships between two records (facts) that may belong to any domain (e.g., a person related to a care site, or one measurement related to another). Each row links fact 1 to fact 2 with a typed relationship. Table is currently empty.

columns.name: domain_concept_id_1
columns.description: Concept ID identifying the OMOP domain of the first fact (fact_id_1), indicating which table/entity that fact comes from (e.g., Person, Visit, Measurement). References the standardized vocabulary CONCEPT table.

columns.name: fact_id_1
columns.description: Identifier (primary key value) of the first record within the table/domain given by domain_concept_id_1.

columns.name: domain_concept_id_2
columns.description: Concept ID identifying the OMOP domain of the second fact (fact_id_2), indicating which table/entity that fact comes from. References the standardized vocabulary CONCEPT table.

columns.name: fact_id_2
columns.description: Identifier (primary key value) of the second record within the table/domain given by domain_concept_id_2.

columns.name: relationship_concept_id
columns.description: Concept ID specifying the type/nature of the relationship from fact 1 to fact 2 (e.g., parent-child, before-after). References the standardized vocabulary CONCEPT table.

location.table_description: OMOP CDM LOCATION table: stores physical address/geographic location records (street, city, state, zip, county) referenced by persons, care sites, and providers. Each row is a unique location identified by location_id.

columns.name: location_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each location record; integer, non-nullable.

columns.name: address_1
columns.description: First line of the street address (e.g., house number and street name).

columns.name: address_2
columns.description: Second line of the street address (e.g., apartment, suite, or unit number).

columns.name: city
columns.description: City or town name of the location.

columns.name: state
columns.description: State or province, typically a 2-character US state abbreviation.

columns.name: zip
columns.description: Postal/ZIP code of the location, stored as text to preserve leading zeros.

columns.name: county
columns.description: County name associated with the location.

columns.name: location_source_value
columns.description: Original/source-system representation of the location as it appeared before standardization.

columns.name: measurement_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each measurement record (distinct_ratio 1.0).

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Foreign key to person.person_id; the patient the measurement was recorded for.

columns.name: measurement_concept_id
columns.description: Standardized OMOP concept ID (FK to concept) identifying the measurement/observable, mapped from LOINC/SNOMED source codes (only ~38% distinct, i.e. ~1670 distinct concepts).

columns.name: measurement_date
columns.description: Calendar date the measurement was taken, ranging 1912-2019.

columns.name: measurement_datetime
columns.description: Timestamp of the measurement; identical to measurement_date with 00:00:00 time component (times not captured).

columns.name: measurement_time
columns.description: Time-of-day field for the measurement, here populated with the date string instead of a time; effectively redundant with measurement_date.

columns.name: measurement_type_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept ID for the provenance/type of the measurement record; constant 5001 (a single source type) across all rows.

columns.name: operator_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept ID for a comparison operator (e.g. =, <, >) applied to the value; populated only with 0 (no value), so unused.

columns.name: value_as_number
columns.description: Numeric result value of the measurement; entirely null in this dataset.

columns.name: value_as_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept ID for a categorical/coded result value; only 0 (no value) here, so unused.

columns.name: unit_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept ID for the measurement unit; only 0 (no value) here, so unused.

columns.name: range_low
columns.description: Lower bound of the normal reference range for the measurement; entirely null here.

columns.name: range_high
columns.description: Upper bound of the normal reference range for the measurement; entirely null here.

columns.name: provider_id
columns.description: FK to the provider responsible for the measurement; only 0 (no value) here, so unused.

columns.name: visit_occurrence_id
columns.description: FK to the visit/encounter during which the measurement occurred (visit_occurrence table); largely distinct per row.

columns.name: visit_detail_id
columns.description: FK to a finer-grained visit detail record; only 0 (no value) here, so unused.

columns.name: measurement_source_value
columns.description: Original source code for the measurement as recorded in source data, e.g. SNOMED codes (117015009) or LOINC codes (8331-1).

columns.name: measurement_source_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept ID corresponding to the source code; matches measurement_concept_id values, FK to concept.

columns.name: unit_source_value
columns.description: Original source text for the measurement unit; entirely null here.

columns.name: value_source_value
columns.description: Original source text for the result value; entirely null here.

measurement.table_description: OMOP Common Data Model MEASUREMENT table: each row is a clinical measurement, lab test, or vital sign recorded for a patient during a visit, identified by standardized and source concept codes (LOINC/SNOMED). In this dataset value/unit/range fields are unpopulated, so only the measurement type, date, patient, and visit are captured.

metadata.table_description: OMOP CDM METADATA table: stores metadata about the CDM instance/dataset (e.g., CDM version, ETL details, vocabulary versions), with each row recording a named metadata item and its value. Empty in this profile (0 rows).

columns.name: metadata_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the CONCEPT table identifying the type of metadata being recorded (the standardized concept for the metadata item).

columns.name: metadata_type_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the CONCEPT table indicating the category/type of the metadata record (e.g., source vs. standardized metadata).

columns.name: name
columns.description: Free-text name/label of the metadata item being recorded.

columns.name: value_as_string
columns.description: The metadata value when represented as a text string.

columns.name: value_as_concept_id
columns.description: The metadata value when represented as a standardized concept; foreign key to the CONCEPT table.

columns.name: metadata_date
columns.description: Date associated with the metadata record (e.g., when the metadata was recorded or applies).

columns.name: metadata_datetime
columns.description: Timestamp associated with the metadata record (date and time without time zone).

note.table_description: OMOP CDM NOTE table: stores unstructured clinical notes (free-text documents) recorded for patients, along with metadata about each note's type, class, encoding, language, and the encounter/provider it is associated with.

columns.name: note_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each clinical note record.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the PERSON table; the patient the note pertains to.

columns.name: note_date
columns.description: Calendar date the note was recorded/entered.

columns.name: note_datetime
columns.description: Date and time the note was recorded; finer-grained version of note_date.

columns.name: note_type_concept_id
columns.description: Concept ID (Type Concept vocabulary) indicating the provenance/source type of the note, e.g. EHR record vs derived.

columns.name: note_class_concept_id
columns.description: Concept ID (typically LOINC document ontology) classifying the kind/category of the clinical document.

columns.name: note_title
columns.description: Free-text title or heading of the note.

columns.name: note_text
columns.description: The full unstructured free-text content of the clinical note.

columns.name: encoding_concept_id
columns.description: Concept ID identifying the character/text encoding used for the note content (e.g. UTF-8).

columns.name: language_concept_id
columns.description: Concept ID identifying the natural language the note is written in.

columns.name: provider_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the PROVIDER table; the clinician who authored or is responsible for the note.

columns.name: visit_occurrence_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the VISIT_OCCURRENCE table; the patient visit/encounter during which the note was created.

columns.name: visit_detail_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the VISIT_DETAIL table; a more granular sub-visit context for the note.

columns.name: note_source_value
columns.description: Original source-system code or value for the note's type/identifier prior to mapping.

note_nlp.table_description: OMOP CDM NOTE_NLP table: stores structured terms and concepts extracted from clinical notes by a natural language processing (NLP) system. Each row represents one NLP-derived term/annotation found within a note.

columns.name: note_nlp_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each NLP annotation/extracted term record.

columns.name: note_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the NOTE table identifying the source clinical note this NLP term was extracted from.

columns.name: section_concept_id
columns.description: Concept ID (OMOP vocabulary) identifying the note section (e.g., Past Medical History) where the term was found.

columns.name: snippet
columns.description: Short text excerpt from the note containing the extracted lexical variant, providing surrounding context.

columns.name: offset
columns.description: Character offset/position of the term within the note text (stored as string).

columns.name: lexical_variant
columns.description: The raw text string of the term as it actually appeared in the note.

columns.name: note_nlp_concept_id
columns.description: Standard OMOP concept ID that the extracted term was mapped to by the NLP system.

columns.name: note_nlp_source_concept_id
columns.description: Source/original concept ID assigned by the NLP system before mapping to a standard concept.

columns.name: nlp_system
columns.description: Name and version of the NLP system/tool that produced the annotation.

columns.name: nlp_date
columns.description: Date the NLP processing of the note was performed.

columns.name: nlp_datetime
columns.description: Timestamp (date and time) the NLP processing was performed.

columns.name: term_exists
columns.description: NLP-derived flag indicating whether the condition/term is asserted to exist (e.g., negation/assertion status).

columns.name: term_temporal
columns.description: NLP-derived temporal context of the term (e.g., present, history, past).

columns.name: term_modifiers
columns.description: NLP-derived modifiers/attributes of the term, often as delimited key-value pairs (e.g., severity, subject).

observation.table_description: OMOP Common Data Model OBSERVATION table: each row records a clinical observation/fact about a patient (e.g., symptoms, social or historical facts) captured during a visit, identified by standardized SNOMED-based concept IDs. Here populated with 1,477 observations for patients across visits from 1910 to 2019.

columns.name: observation_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key; unique integer identifier for each observation record.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Foreign key to person.person_id identifying the patient the observation pertains to.

columns.name: observation_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to concept.concept_id giving the standardized concept for what was observed (OMOP/SNOMED clinical concept).

columns.name: observation_date
columns.description: Calendar date on which the observation was recorded.

columns.name: observation_datetime
columns.description: Timestamp of the observation; mirrors observation_date with time set to midnight (no time-of-day precision).

columns.name: observation_type_concept_id
columns.description: Concept ID indicating the provenance/type of the observation record; only two values (38000276, 38000280) present.

columns.name: value_as_number
columns.description: Numeric result value of the observation; entirely null in this dataset.

columns.name: value_as_string
columns.description: Textual result value of the observation; entirely null in this dataset.

columns.name: value_as_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to concept.concept_id for a result expressed as a coded concept; populated only with 0 (no value).

columns.name: qualifier_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to concept.concept_id qualifying the observation; populated only with 0 (no value).

columns.name: unit_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to concept.concept_id for the unit of value_as_number; populated only with 0 (no value).

columns.name: provider_id
columns.description: Identifier of the provider associated with the observation; populated only with 0 (unknown).

columns.name: visit_occurrence_id
columns.description: Identifier of the visit (encounter) during which the observation was made; foreign key to visit_occurrence.

columns.name: visit_detail_id
columns.description: Identifier of the visit detail subrecord; populated only with 0 (unused).

columns.name: observation_source_value
columns.description: Original source code for the observation, here SNOMED CT numeric codes (e.g., 428251008).

columns.name: observation_source_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to concept.concept_id for the source concept; matches observation_concept_id, indicating the source code mapped directly to a standard concept.

columns.name: unit_source_value
columns.description: Original source text for the unit of measure; entirely null in this dataset.

columns.name: qualifier_source_value
columns.description: Original source text for the observation qualifier; entirely null in this dataset.

observation_period.table_description: OMOP Common Data Model OBSERVATION_PERIOD table: each row defines a continuous span of time during which a person is observed in the source data, with a start and end date. Contains 5,343 periods linked to patients in the PERSON table.

columns.name: observation_period_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each observation period record.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Foreign key to person.person_id identifying the patient this observation period belongs to.

columns.name: observation_period_start_date
columns.description: Date on which the patient's observation period begins (earliest date of observation in source data); ranges 1909-07-07 to 2019-05-27.

columns.name: observation_period_end_date
columns.description: Date on which the patient's observation period ends (latest date of observation); ranges 1925-09-07 to 2019-07-01.

columns.name: period_type_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP standard concept ID describing the provenance/type of the observation period; constant value 44814724 ('Period covering healthcare encounters' / EHR-derived period type).

payer_plan_period.table_description: OMOP CDM PAYER_PLAN_PERIOD table: each row records a continuous span of time during which a person was covered by a specific health insurance payer and plan (and optionally a sponsor/family). The table is currently empty.

columns.name: payer_plan_period_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each payer/plan coverage period record.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the PERSON table; identifies the individual covered by this payer plan period.

columns.name: payer_plan_period_start_date
columns.description: Start date of the coverage period under this payer/plan.

columns.name: payer_plan_period_end_date
columns.description: End date of the coverage period under this payer/plan.

columns.name: payer_concept_id
columns.description: Standard OMOP concept ID identifying the insurance payer (e.g., Medicare, Medicaid, private insurer).

columns.name: payer_source_value
columns.description: Verbatim payer name/code as recorded in the source data.

columns.name: payer_source_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept ID representing the payer code in its original source vocabulary.

columns.name: plan_concept_id
columns.description: Standard OMOP concept ID identifying the specific insurance plan or benefit type under the payer.

columns.name: plan_source_value
columns.description: Verbatim insurance plan name/code as recorded in the source data.

columns.name: plan_source_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept ID representing the plan code in its original source vocabulary.

columns.name: sponsor_concept_id
columns.description: Standard OMOP concept ID identifying the plan sponsor (e.g., employer or organization funding the plan).

columns.name: sponsor_source_value
columns.description: Verbatim sponsor name/code as recorded in the source data.

columns.name: sponsor_source_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept ID representing the sponsor code in its original source vocabulary.

columns.name: family_source_value
columns.description: Source value identifying the family or subscriber/dependent grouping for the insurance coverage.

columns.name: stop_reason_concept_id
columns.description: Standard OMOP concept ID describing the reason the coverage period ended (e.g., plan changed, lost eligibility).

columns.name: stop_reason_source_value
columns.description: Verbatim reason-for-termination code/text from the source data.

columns.name: stop_reason_source_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept ID representing the stop-reason code in its original source vocabulary.

procedure_occurrence.table_description: OMOP Common Data Model PROCEDURE_OCCURRENCE table: each row records a single procedure performed on a patient on a given date, with standardized and source procedure codes. Here populated with 37,409 procedures for ~2,041 patients spanning 1917–2019.

columns.name: procedure_occurrence_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each procedure occurrence record.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Foreign key to person.person_id; the patient who underwent the procedure.

columns.name: procedure_concept_id
columns.description: Standardized procedure concept ID (FK to concept.concept_id), typically a SNOMED/standard vocabulary procedure concept. Values mirror procedure_source_concept_id here.

columns.name: procedure_date
columns.description: Calendar date the procedure was performed.

columns.name: procedure_datetime
columns.description: Date and time the procedure was performed; times are all midnight, so effectively equal to procedure_date.

columns.name: procedure_type_concept_id
columns.description: Concept ID describing the provenance/source type of the procedure record (FK to concept). Constant 38000275, indicating a single source type for all rows.

columns.name: modifier_concept_id
columns.description: Standardized concept ID for a procedure modifier (FK to concept). Always 0, i.e. no modifier recorded.

columns.name: quantity
columns.description: Quantity/number of times the procedure was performed; entirely null (not populated).

columns.name: provider_id
columns.description: Reference to the provider who performed the procedure; entirely null (not populated).

columns.name: visit_occurrence_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the visit_occurrence during which the procedure took place.

columns.name: visit_detail_id
columns.description: Reference to a finer-grained visit_detail record; always 0, i.e. not populated.

columns.name: procedure_source_value
columns.description: Original source procedure code as text, appears to be SNOMED CT codes (e.g. 180256009).

columns.name: procedure_source_concept_id
columns.description: Concept ID mapped from the source procedure code (FK to concept); matches procedure_concept_id in this data.

columns.name: modifier_source_value
columns.description: Original source value for the procedure modifier; entirely null (not populated).

provider.table_description: OMOP CDM PROVIDER table: records individual healthcare providers (clinicians) who deliver care, including their identifiers, specialty, and demographics. Each row is one provider.

columns.name: provider_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each provider.

columns.name: provider_name
columns.description: Free-text name of the provider (clinician or person).

columns.name: npi
columns.description: National Provider Identifier (US 10-digit NPI), stored as text.

columns.name: dea
columns.description: Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) registration number authorizing controlled-substance prescribing, stored as text.

columns.name: specialty_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the standardized OMOP concept representing the provider's specialty.

columns.name: care_site_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the CARE_SITE table identifying the provider's primary care site/location.

columns.name: year_of_birth
columns.description: Provider's year of birth (4-digit year).

columns.name: gender_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the standardized OMOP concept representing the provider's gender.

columns.name: provider_source_value
columns.description: Original provider identifier/value from the source system.

columns.name: specialty_source_value
columns.description: Provider specialty as represented in the source data (raw text/code).

columns.name: specialty_source_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the OMOP concept representing the source specialty code before standardization.

columns.name: gender_source_value
columns.description: Provider gender as represented in the source data (raw value).

columns.name: gender_source_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the OMOP concept representing the source gender code before standardization.

relationship.table_description: OMOP CDM vocabulary metadata table defining the types of relationships that can exist between concepts (e.g., SNOMED "Has component", "Direct procedure site of"). Each row is a distinct relationship type, including hierarchy flags and a pointer to its inverse relationship.

columns.name: relationship_id
columns.description: Primary key: short unique text identifier/label for the relationship type (e.g., 'Has kind', 'Has component', 'Source - RxNorm eq'). Distinct per row.

columns.name: relationship_name
columns.description: Full human-readable name of the relationship, typically with source vocabulary in parentheses (e.g., 'Direct procedure site of (SNOMED)', 'VA Class to NDFRT equivalent (NDF-RT)').

columns.name: is_hierarchical
columns.description: Boolean flag (stored as '0'/'1' text) indicating whether the relationship defines a hierarchy between concepts.

columns.name: defines_ancestry
columns.description: Boolean flag (stored as '0'/'1' text) indicating whether the hierarchical relationship contributes to the concept_ancestor (ancestor/descendant) computation.

columns.name: reverse_relationship_id
columns.description: Foreign key to relationship_id of the inverse relationship (e.g., 'Has kind' paired with its reverse). References this same table's relationship_id.

columns.name: relationship_concept_id
columns.description: Integer concept_id (foreign key to the CONCEPT table) that represents this relationship as a standardized concept.

source_to_concept_map.table_description: OMOP CDM vocabulary table mapping source codes (from local/source vocabularies) to standard concepts. Each row defines a mapping from a source_code to a target_concept_id, with validity dates.

columns.name: source_code
columns.description: The source vocabulary code being mapped (string, as it appears in the source data).

columns.name: source_concept_id
columns.description: Concept id representing the source code; references CONCEPT.concept_id, or 0 if the source code is not represented as a concept.

columns.name: source_vocabulary_id
columns.description: Identifier of the vocabulary that the source_code belongs to; references VOCABULARY.vocabulary_id.

columns.name: source_code_description
columns.description: Optional free-text description of the source code.

columns.name: target_concept_id
columns.description: Concept id the source code maps to; references CONCEPT.concept_id (the standard/target concept).

columns.name: target_vocabulary_id
columns.description: Identifier of the vocabulary of the target concept; references VOCABULARY.vocabulary_id.

columns.name: valid_start_date
columns.description: Date when this source-to-target mapping became valid.

columns.name: valid_end_date
columns.description: Date when this mapping ceased to be valid (often a far-future placeholder if still valid).

columns.name: invalid_reason
columns.description: Reason a mapping is no longer valid: typically 'D' (deleted) or 'U' (updated); NULL if currently valid.

specimen.table_description: OMOP CDM SPECIMEN table: records biological specimens (e.g., blood, tissue, swab samples) collected from a person, including the specimen type, collection date, quantity, and anatomical/disease context.

columns.name: specimen_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each specimen record.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the PERSON table; the patient from whom the specimen was collected.

columns.name: specimen_concept_id
columns.description: Standard concept ID (OMOP vocabulary) identifying the type of specimen collected, e.g., serum, urine, tissue.

columns.name: specimen_type_concept_id
columns.description: Concept ID indicating the provenance/source type of the specimen record (how the specimen data was captured).

columns.name: specimen_date
columns.description: Date the specimen was collected.

columns.name: specimen_datetime
columns.description: Date and time the specimen was collected (timestamp without time zone).

columns.name: quantity
columns.description: Numeric amount/volume of the specimen collected, expressed in the unit given by unit_concept_id.

columns.name: unit_concept_id
columns.description: Standard concept ID for the unit of measure of the specimen quantity (e.g., mL, g).

columns.name: anatomic_site_concept_id
columns.description: Standard concept ID for the anatomical location from which the specimen was obtained.

columns.name: disease_status_concept_id
columns.description: Standard concept ID describing the disease status of the tissue at collection (e.g., tumor, normal).

columns.name: specimen_source_id
columns.description: Source system identifier for the specimen record as found in the originating data.

columns.name: specimen_source_value
columns.description: Original (unmapped) source code or text describing the specimen type.

columns.name: unit_source_value
columns.description: Original source code or text for the quantity's unit of measure.

columns.name: anatomic_site_source_value
columns.description: Original source code or text for the anatomical site of collection.

columns.name: disease_status_source_value
columns.description: Original source code or text for the disease status of the specimen.

visit_detail.table_description: OMOP CDM visit_detail table: records detailed sub-visits or movements within a higher-level visit occurrence (e.g., transfers between wards/units), one row per visit detail event for a person.

columns.name: visit_detail_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each visit detail record.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the person (patient) the visit detail belongs to.

columns.name: visit_detail_concept_id
columns.description: Standard OMOP concept ID classifying the type/setting of the visit detail (e.g., inpatient ward, ICU, outpatient).

columns.name: visit_detail_start_date
columns.description: Date the visit detail (sub-visit) began.

columns.name: visit_detail_start_datetime
columns.description: Timestamp the visit detail began; nullable, more precise than start_date.

columns.name: visit_detail_end_date
columns.description: Date the visit detail (sub-visit) ended.

columns.name: visit_detail_end_datetime
columns.description: Timestamp the visit detail ended; nullable.

columns.name: visit_detail_type_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept ID indicating the provenance/source type of the visit detail record (e.g., derived from EHR, claim).

columns.name: provider_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the provider associated with this visit detail; nullable.

columns.name: care_site_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the care site (location/unit) where the visit detail occurred; nullable.

columns.name: visit_detail_source_value
columns.description: Original source-system text/code for the visit detail before mapping to a standard concept.

columns.name: visit_detail_source_concept_id
columns.description: Source (non-standard) OMOP concept ID for the visit detail as represented in the source vocabulary.

columns.name: admitting_source_value
columns.description: Original source text/code describing where the patient was admitted from.

columns.name: admitting_source_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept ID for the admitting source/origin of the patient.

columns.name: discharge_to_source_value
columns.description: Original source text/code describing the discharge destination.

columns.name: discharge_to_concept_id
columns.description: OMOP concept ID for the discharge destination/disposition.

columns.name: preceding_visit_detail_id
columns.description: Self-referencing foreign key to the immediately preceding visit_detail record for the same person.

columns.name: visit_detail_parent_id
columns.description: Self-referencing foreign key to the parent visit_detail record, representing hierarchical nesting of sub-visits.

columns.name: visit_occurrence_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the visit_occurrence this detail belongs to (the encompassing higher-level visit).

visit_occurrence.table_description: OMOP Common Data Model VISIT_OCCURRENCE table: each row is a single patient encounter/visit (here all coded as inpatient hospitalizations), with start/end dates, the visiting patient, and source/standardized concept references. Populated with 1,037 visits spanning 1923–2019.

columns.name: visit_occurrence_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each visit/encounter record.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: Foreign key to person.person_id identifying the patient for this visit; 0.861 distinct ratio indicates some patients have multiple visits.

columns.name: visit_concept_id
columns.description: Standardized concept ID (FK to concept) classifying the visit type; constant value 9201 = 'Inpatient Visit'.

columns.name: visit_start_date
columns.description: Calendar date the visit began.

columns.name: visit_start_datetime
columns.description: Timestamp the visit began; time component is always 00:00:00, so effectively redundant with visit_start_date.

columns.name: visit_end_date
columns.description: Calendar date the visit ended/patient was discharged.

columns.name: visit_end_datetime
columns.description: Timestamp the visit ended; time component is always 00:00:00, mirroring visit_end_date.

columns.name: visit_type_concept_id
columns.description: Standardized concept ID indicating the provenance/type of the visit record; constant 44818517 ('Visit derived from encounter on claim' / similar EHR-derived source).

columns.name: provider_id
columns.description: Intended FK to the provider responsible for the visit; entirely NULL in this dataset.

columns.name: care_site_id
columns.description: Intended FK to the care site/facility where the visit occurred; entirely NULL in this dataset.

columns.name: visit_source_value
columns.description: Source system identifier for the visit, here a UUID string from the originating EHR/dataset.

columns.name: visit_source_concept_id
columns.description: Standardized concept ID for the source visit code (FK to concept); always 0, meaning unmapped/none.

columns.name: admitting_source_concept_id
columns.description: Standardized concept ID for the admitting source/origin of admission (FK to concept); always 0 (unmapped).

columns.name: admitting_source_value
columns.description: Source text describing where the patient was admitted from; entirely NULL here.

columns.name: discharge_to_concept_id
columns.description: Standardized concept ID for discharge disposition/destination (FK to concept); always 0 (unmapped).

columns.name: discharge_to_source_value
columns.description: Source text describing the patient's discharge destination; entirely NULL here.

columns.name: preceding_visit_occurrence_id
columns.description: Self-referencing reference to the patient's immediately prior visit_occurrence_id, linking sequential visits.

vocabulary.table_description: OMOP CDM VOCABULARY reference table: each row defines a controlled medical vocabulary/terminology (e.g., RxNorm, NDC, ICD9CM, UCUM) used as the source of concepts in the standardized vocabularies.

columns.name: vocabulary_id
columns.description: Short unique identifier/abbreviation for the vocabulary (e.g., 'UCUM', 'NFC', 'Cost'). Primary key.

columns.name: vocabulary_name
columns.description: Full descriptive name of the vocabulary, e.g., 'National Drug Code (FDA and manufacturers)', 'WHO Anatomic Therapeutic Chemical Classification'.

columns.name: vocabulary_reference
columns.description: Source/reference for the vocabulary: a URL, owning organization, or origin note (e.g., 'OMOP generated', NLM/RxNorm URLs, 'IMS proprietary').

columns.name: vocabulary_version
columns.description: Free-text version/release identifier of the vocabulary as loaded (e.g., 'RXNORM 2018-08-12', 'ICD9CM v32 master descriptions'). Nullable (~33% null).

columns.name: vocabulary_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the CONCEPT table identifying the concept that represents this vocabulary itself; integer OMOP concept_id.
